title: Taste pair
description: Structured storage contract for taste/pairs.jsonl. One pair = one user-picked variant plus the rejected siblings from a single /variants session. Consumed by FSPO few-shot injection in context-inference.md Step 4 — the retrieval stage selects top-N pairs under similar context and injects them as in-prompt taste anchors.

chosen.description: The variant the user picked. variant_index refers to the position in the /variants output (0 = variant A, 1 = variant B, 2 = variant C).

rejected.description: Variants the user saw but did NOT pick in the same round. At least one entry — a pair with no alternatives is not a taste signal, it's a single pick.

context.description: Brief metadata that lets the FSPO retriever find similar contexts. brief_summary is LLM-compressed to ≤ 60 tokens so it stays cheap to include as a few-shot example.

brief_summary.description: One-line summary of the generation brief. Example: 'dashboard for a fintech, Swedish, power-user density'.

product_archetype.description: Detected product type (e.g. 'fintech', 'saas-b2b', 'ecommerce'). From the StyleBrief inference output.

component_type.description: Primary component requested (e.g. 'dashboard', 'landing', 'pricing'). Matches the keys in context-inference.md Step 2.5.

audience_density.description: The density target from the StyleBrief.

motion_tier.description: The motion tier from the StyleBrief at capture time.

brand_archetype.description: One of the 12 brand archetypes (Ruler, Sage, Explorer, Creator, Innocent, Hero, Caregiver, Jester, Lover, Outlaw, Magician, Everyman).

created_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the pick.
